blocks.content: Shirone floated through a dark space.
In some places, countless stars were visible, while in others, only endless darkness stretched on.
In the darkness, it felt as though time had stopped, and a fear that he might never escape crept over him.
One by one, stars began to appear and converge toward a single point.
It felt as though space itself was contracting.
He shuddered at the unimaginable scale of the stars.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He couldn't fathom what would happen when they finally collided at that last point.
But the collision never came.
The stars began to collapse, disintegrating into dust before being sucked away.
Time was flowing backward.
By the time he realized it, Shirone's physical body had already decomposed and vanished.
Yet Shirone did not disappear.
Though he didn't know what this last remnant of his existence was, he remained conscious as he journeyed toward the beginning of the world.
A writhing, vibrating string of light came into view.
Within it, light, time, gravity, and universal forces were unified.
Finally, upon reaching the end—or rather, the beginning—the entire world was filled with light.
The presence that made up Shirone began to fade, and an overwhelming sense of unity washed over him.
It was a unification akin to a colossal explosion.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Aaaah!
Shirone screamed and bolted upright in bed.
His clothes were drenched in cold sweat, as if he'd been caught in the rain.
The surroundings were silent, and the moonlight cast a desolate hue over the windowsill.
The familiar scent of books lingered in the air.
On the desk, books about photon theory lay scattered in disarray.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hah… Another nightmare…?
He gulped down water from a kettle, then opened the window to let the early summer breeze dry his sweat.
Only then could he calm down enough to sit in his chair.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why does this keep happening every night? What's wrong with me?
Ever since experiencing the Immortal Function during the Speed Gun test, he'd been having similar dreams.
Though not exactly nightmares, the emotions he felt in them were no less terrifying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh…
Shirone hugged his trembling body and rubbed his arms.
It felt as though something fundamental within him had gone awry.
The problem was that this phenomenon wasn't limited to dreams—it was seeping into reality.
One of his senses had become unnaturally sharp.
But he couldn't figure out what kind of information that sense was even receiving.
It wasn't that his sense of smell or touch had improved.
His vision and hearing were unchanged, too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't tell me… it's something like a ghost?
The sound of a piano signaled the arrival of morning.
Seeing the dawn break outside, Shirone headed to the bathroom to wash up.
With the advanced class's practical evaluations over, normal classes would resume today.
As the warm water touched him, the strange sensations gradually faded.
If this persisted all day, he'd surely suffer a nervous breakdown.
Partly relieved, Shirone packed his bag.
With the exam period over, the school regained its lively atmosphere.
Students who had once competed fiercely now chatted cheerfully as if nothing had happened.
The recent topic of discussion was Shirone's Speed Gun test.
Unlike written exams, practical evaluations had a sporting element, so it was natural for students to be excited when someone delivered a spectacular performance.
Usually, each class had its own standout students, but the impact of the Speed Gun test on the first day was so overwhelming that everyone else was overshadowed.
For the first time in the magic academy's history, two students had scored in the 1200-point range.
The difference between Shirone and Iruki's scores was a mere six points, and the match had been an unbelievable comeback.
With such a legendary showdown happening on the first day, even outstanding performances from later participants couldn't compare.
Shirone had skyrocketed to stardom.
Though he'd been famous since enrollment, it had been as Amy's boyfriend or a promising rising star.
Now, he was undeniably the face of Alpheas Magic Academy.
Even on his way to class, Shirone was greeted warmly by students—some of whom were seniors from Class Four.
In a magic academy with an early advancement system, the hierarchy between upper and lowerclassmen was strict because progression was difficult.
But given Shirone's historic achievement, treating him as just another underclassman felt awkward.
In hindsight, Amy's decision to acknowledge him as an equal had been remarkably foresighted.
By the time he reached the advanced class building, Shirone was already exhausted from exchanging greetings with everyone.

blocks.speaker: Class Six girls
blocks.content: Huh? It's Shirone-senpai! Hello, senpai!

blocks.speaker: Class Six girls
blocks.content: Shirone-senpai, I'm your fan! Can I follow you around from now on?
Originally, they'd been the most resentful of Shirone's rapid advancement.
Jumping from Class Seven to Class Five had left them feeling slighted.
But now, even Class Four seniors saw him as a future rival among the graduating class, so they had no choice but to change their attitudes.

blocks.speaker: Class Six girls
blocks.content: Senpai, what are you doing after class today? We're holding a magic demonstration with friends—want to join?

blocks.speaker: Class Six girls
blocks.content: We're going out on the holiday. Senpai, come with us! I'll be your partner.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sorry. I haven't been feeling well lately.
Between the nightmares and the eerie sensations that struck him even while awake, he didn't want to go anywhere.
Knowing about the insurmountable mountain named Amy, the girls didn't press him and left, promising to invite him next time.

blocks.speaker: Classmate
blocks.content: Shirone, Shirone, Shirone! Shirone, Shirone!
The moment he opened the classroom door, a classmate yanked him inside.
Without even a chance to set down his bag, Shirone was dragged in, bewildered by the stares of his friends.

blocks.speaker: Classmate
blocks.content: What's going on? What happened?

blocks.speaker: Classmate
blocks.content: The semester's almost over—join our research club. We're making one just for Class Five students. The final project is a free topic anyway. It'll save time and be fun. So, join us.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm.
He'd received many offers, but if he were to join a research club, he'd always assumed it would be the Supernatural Parapsychology Club, where Neid and Iruki were members.

blocks.speaker: Classmate
blocks.content: Shirone, come on, think about it. The reason you haven't joined a club is because of personal study, right? There are plenty of students here like you. But we still have to do the final project. So we're making a temporary one. Since it's not a major grading factor, we can finish it quickly and disband. That way, you'll have more time for personal study.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah. That makes sense.
It was a solid strategy.
The final project was a free topic, so it carried less weight than written or practical exams.
Joining an existing club just to complete the project and then leaving would be shameless.
Forming a temporary club with other unaffiliated students was a clever workaround.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hey! What are you scheming? Shirone's off-limits! Shirone's mine, got it?
Neid hid Shirone behind his back and shouted.
The students stared at him in disbelief.
Those who knew Neid's true intentions glared at him.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: That's ridiculous. You just want to drag him into that weird club of yours, right? Shirone, you absolutely cannot join that one. Even if you don't join ours, pick any other club. In all my years here, I've never heard of Neid actually completing a final project.
The Supernatural Parapsychology Club boasted a long tradition, but its official activities were rare.
Most students didn't even know Neid was part of a club.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Huh? Neid, you're in a club?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hmph. Of course. Did you really think the school's most popular guy wouldn't have a club? Anyway, Shirone's coming with me.
Neid grabbed Shirone's wrist and headed for the door.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Hey, Shirone! Think carefully! Don't you dare join that club! And Neid, stop interfering with Shirone's studies and let him join a normal one!

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hahahaha! Your words mean nothing! Shirone's mind is already filled with only me! We've already crossed the forbidden line!
Shirone's face turned pale.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait, Neid. What are you talking about…?
Neid didn't give him a chance to argue and slammed the door shut.
The students in the classroom stared blankly at the scene.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: …What did he just say?
This is bad. That bastard's seriously planning to drag Shirone into his club.
We can't let him get swayed.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Isn't it too late? He's already being dragged around. And where the hell is he taking him? Class is about to start.
The moment they stepped into the hallway, Shirone shook off Neid's hand.
He knew it was a joke, but the eerie feeling lingered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey! Why would you say something like that?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hahaha! It's not wrong, is it? You're the only one who's seen the true nature of the Supernatural Parapsychology Club.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The true nature? There was nothing to see.
Neid glanced around at the people passing by before pushing Shirone into a crouch.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: First, sit down for a sec.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. Stop being so pushy.
Shirone crouched and leaned against the wall.
After checking their surroundings again, Neid lowered his voice.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: You're the only one who's actually been inside the club. So you absolutely can't tell anyone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who's even going to ask?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Anyway! The higher-ups are already talking about disbanding the club because of the lack of members.
We have to keep a low profile, so don't even mention the word 'supernatural.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Got it. I wasn't planning to.

To Shirone, the Supernatural Parapsychology Club seemed like a lost cause, but its true value was known only to a shadowy group of researchers who were always looking for an opportunity to exploit it.
The teachers, meanwhile, were desperate to disband it.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: By the way, are you really joining the club?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I was considering it. Why? Is there a reason I shouldn't?
Neid had known about the strategy of forming temporary clubs at the end of the semester for a long time.
While it was efficient, it didn't seem like the right fit for Shirone's situation.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Of course. Even if the final project isn't a major grading factor, teaming up with just anyone is a waste.
Besides, you're aiming for the top of the school, right?
Every point matters.
You'd be better off working alone or joining a proper club.
In Class Four, where students aimed for the graduating class, perfect scorers in certain subjects weren't uncommon, so even a one-point difference in the final project could be significant.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm. But doing the final project alone would take too much time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Neid. What if… I joined the Supernatural Parapsychology Club?
Neid's face lit up.
If Shirone joined, the club would have its three musketeers.
Though they often hung out together, Iruki frequently skipped class, leaving their group incomplete.
But Neid couldn't immediately agree.
Alpheas Magic Academy had been founded 45 years ago.
While most students took graduation exams and entered society, there was another hidden society where all sorts of people engaged in bizarre activities.
The Supernatural Parapsychology Club was one such place.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hmm. Are you sure? Our club is… closer to the shadows. Supernatural parapsychology is already niche.
I'd love to have you, but honestly, I wouldn't recommend it to a friend.
Shirone had guessed from his classmates' reactions that Neid's club had secrets.
But he didn't want to join a club just for grades.
Supernatural parapsychology had been an interest of his since childhood.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not just about that. I've been… feeling off lately.
I was actually going to ask you guys about it.
Maybe supernatural parapsychology could explain it.
